description: Trigger discrimination for the worker EXECUTION model (this skill) vs the message TRANSPORT it rides (streaming-event-architect — THE hard-pinned seam), the measurement of capacity under load (performance-test-harness / load-test-planner), and the synchronous protected write path (command-gateway-architect).

cases.prompt: Design how our background workers run jobs: idempotency and retry-with-backoff per job, visibility timeouts, a dead-letter queue with an owner, resumable long jobs, and fairness so one tenant can't monopolize the workers.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — retry, visibility timeout, DLQ ownership, checkpointing, and worker fairness are execution mechanics: what RUNS the work, not the pipe it travels on nor how fast it goes under load.
cases.reason: The worker execution contract and per-tenant fairness are this skill. It assumes a transport rather than designing one, and it designs rather than measures.

cases.prompt: Design the queue/stream layer itself: stream vs work-queue per flow, partition keys and ordering scope, consumer groups, at-least-once delivery semantics, retention vs compaction, and CDC ingestion.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse — HARD PIN) — partition keys, ordering scope, delivery semantics, retention, and CDC are transport design. Whether a worker retries or dead-letters is not mentioned; the pipe is the subject.
cases.reason: The message transport (topics, partitions, delivery semantics, CDC) is streaming-event-architect. Jobs ride that transport; this skill designs the worker on top of it. Transport choice does not transfer to the execution model.

cases.prompt: We need to know whether our job system survives Black-Friday-scale bursts: model realistic enqueue traffic across tenants and data volumes and find where it degrades.
cases.reason: Planning realistic load to find the degradation point MEASURES the system; load-test-planner (with performance-test-harness for instrumentation) owns that. This skill designs the model that gets measured, and does not assert its own capacity.

cases.prompt: Design the single server-side path every protected mutation flows through: validate, authenticate the actor from the token, authorize, derive tenant scope, dedup, execute, and audit — synchronously, returning the result to the caller.
cases.reason: A synchronous, authorized, audited write returned to the caller is the command gateway. Background jobs are for work deliberately taken OFF the request path, not for the request-time protected write itself.
